description: Security AI Agent — Conditional Access Policy Catalogue. Defines every policy in the Foundation and Hardening layers with framework control mappings, prerequisites, and deployment guidance.

foundation.description: 16 policies. No Intune or MDCA required. Deploy to all clients at onboarding.
foundation.deployment_order: CAP001-004 first (no user impact, enable immediately), then CAU001-012 in report-only

policies.name: CAP001-All-BlockLegacyAuthentication-v1.0
policies.title: Block Legacy Authentication
policies.description: Blocks all legacy auth protocols (Basic Auth, NTLM, Digest) which cannot enforce MFA
policies.user_impact: none

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — authentication controls; legacy auth cannot support MFA requirements
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.5 Secure Authentication — legacy protocols do not support modern authentication
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.8 Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services; CIS 6.3 Require MFA for externally-exposed applications
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft Identity Secure Score: Block legacy authentication
framework_controls.ncsc: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Secure configuration — disable unnecessary authentication methods
framework_controls.cisa: CISA MFA guidance: disable authentication methods that cannot enforce MFA

policies.name: CAP002-All-BlockExchangeActiveSync-v1.0
policies.title: Block Exchange ActiveSync
policies.description: Blocks Exchange ActiveSync which bypasses modern authentication and CA policies
policies.user_impact: none — modern mail clients unaffected

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — authentication controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.5 Secure Authentication
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.8 Disable Unnecessary Services
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft Identity Secure Score: Block legacy authentication

policies.name: CAP003-All-BlockDeviceCodeFlow-v1.0
policies.title: Block Device Code Flow
policies.description: Blocks device code flow authentication which is commonly abused in phishing attacks
policies.user_impact: none for standard users

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — multi-factor authentication and privileged access
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.5 Secure Authentication
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended CA policy: Block device code flow

policies.name: CAP004-All-BlockAuthenticationTransfer-v1.0
policies.title: Block Authentication Transfer
policies.description: Blocks authentication transfer flows used in QR code phishing attacks
policies.user_impact: none

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — authentication controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.5 Secure Authentication
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended CA policy: Block authentication transfer

policies.name: CAU001-All-RequireMFAAllUsers-v1.0
policies.title: Require MFA for All Users
policies.description: Requires MFA for all internal users on all applications and client types
policies.user_impact: high — all users must register MFA before enablement

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — multi-factor authentication is explicitly required for all users accessing network and information systems
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.5 Secure Authentication — multi-factor authentication for all access to sensitive systems
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications (IG1); CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access (IG1)
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft Identity Secure Score: Require MFA for all users (highest impact action)
framework_controls.ncsc: NCSC Cyber Essentials Plus: Multi-factor authentication for all user accounts
framework_controls.cisa: CISA MFA guidance: Require MFA for all users

policies.name: CAU002-Admins-RequirePhishingResistantMFA-v1.0
policies.title: Require Phishing-Resistant MFA for Admins
policies.description: Requires FIDO2 or Authenticator passkey for all privileged role holders — stronger than standard MFA
policies.target: Global Admin, Security Admin, SharePoint Admin, Exchange Admin, CA Admin, User Admin, Privileged Role Admin, Privileged Auth Admin
policies.default_state: report-only → enable after phishing-resistant MFA rollout
policies.user_impact: admins must register FIDO2 key or passkey
prerequisites: FIDO2 keys or Microsoft Authenticator passkeys deployed to admins

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — explicitly requires MFA for privileged access; phishing-resistant preferred for essential entities
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights — enhanced controls for privileged accounts; A.8.5 Secure Authentication
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access (IG1) — highest priority control
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft Identity Secure Score: Require phishing-resistant MFA for admins
framework_controls.ncsc: NCSC guidance on admin account security: strongest available MFA for privileged accounts
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA: required for privileged users

policies.name: CAU003-All-RequireMFAAdminPortals-v1.0
policies.title: Require MFA for Admin Portals
policies.description: Requires MFA for all users accessing Azure portal, Entra admin centre, and Exchange admin
policies.user_impact: medium — affects any user accessing management portals

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — MFA for privileged access to management systems
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights; A.5.15 Access Control
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: MFA for all admin portal access

policies.name: CAU004-Guests-RequireMFA-v1.0
policies.title: Require MFA for Guests
policies.description: Requires MFA for all B2B guest and external users
policies.user_impact: medium — guests must register MFA

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — MFA applies to all users including external
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.15 Access Control; A.5.18 Access Rights — third party access controls
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA for External Access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: MFA for all guest users

policies.name: CAU005-All-BlockHighRiskSignIns-v1.0
policies.title: Block High Risk Sign-ins
policies.description: Blocks sign-ins detected as high risk by Entra ID Protection
policies.user_impact: low — only affects high-risk sign-in attempts

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(b) — incident handling; Article 21(2)(i) — authentication controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.16 Monitoring Activities; A.5.25 Assessment of Information Security Events
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA; CIS 13.4 Perform Traffic Filtering

policies.name: CAU006-All-BlockHighRiskUsers-v1.0
policies.title: Block High Risk Users
policies.description: Blocks sign-ins from users flagged as high risk by Entra ID Protection
policies.user_impact: low — only blocks compromised accounts

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(b) — incident handling and containment
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.16 Monitoring Activities; A.5.25 Assessment of Information Security Events
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA; CIS 5.3 Disable Dormant Accounts

policies.name: CAU007-All-MediumRiskRequireMFA-v1.0
policies.title: Medium Risk Sign-in Requires MFA
policies.description: Requires MFA step-up for medium risk sign-ins rather than blocking
policies.user_impact: low — step-up MFA for suspicious sign-ins

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(b) — incident handling; Article 21(2)(i) — risk-based authentication
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.16 Monitoring Activities
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA

policies.name: CAU008-Admins-SignInFrequency4Hours-v1.0
policies.title: Admin Session Timeout — 4 Hours
policies.description: Forces admin re-authentication every 4 hours to limit session hijacking exposure
policies.user_impact: medium — admins re-authenticate every 4 hours in browser

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — privileged access controls and session management
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights; A.5.15 Access Control
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: admin session lifetime controls
framework_controls.ncsc: NCSC admin device guidance: session timeout requirements

policies.name: CAU009-Admins-DisableBrowserPersistence-v1.0
policies.title: Disable Browser Persistence for Admins
policies.description: Prevents browser from saving admin sessions — forces re-auth on browser restart
policies.user_impact: medium — admins must sign in each browser session

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — privileged access session controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: disable browser persistence for admins

policies.name: CAU010-All-RequireMFADeviceJoinRegistration-v1.0
policies.title: Require MFA for Device Join/Registration
policies.description: Requires MFA when users join or register devices — prevents unauthorised device registration
policies.user_impact: low — only during device registration

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — authentication controls for device access
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.15 Access Control; A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 1.1 Establish and Maintain Detailed Inventory of Enterprise Assets

policies.name: CAU011-All-RequirePhishingResistantMFAAllUsers-v1.0
policies.title: Require Phishing-Resistant MFA — All Users (Future State)
policies.description: Long-term target state — requires FIDO2/passkey for all users. Deploys report-only until passkey rollout complete. Eventually replaces CAU001.
policies.default_state: report-only — enable only after full passkey rollout
policies.user_impact: high — all users must register FIDO2 or passkey
prerequisites: Full FIDO2/passkey rollout to all users

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — phishing-resistant MFA is the gold standard
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.5 Secure Authentication
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.3 Require MFA
framework_controls.cisa: CISA Phishing-Resistant MFA: target state for all users

policies.name: CAU012-All-TAPSecurityInfoRegistration-v1.0
policies.title: Restrict Security Info Registration via TAP
policies.description: Requires MFA to register security information — prevents attackers registering their own MFA methods on compromised accounts
policies.user_impact: low — only during initial MFA setup
prerequisites: Temporary Access Pass (TAP) configured in tenant

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — authentication controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.17 Authentication Information — managing authentication credentials securely
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: secure MFA registration with TAP

hardening.description: 13 policies across 3 sub-layers. Requires Intune and/or Named Locations and/or MDCA.

policies.name: CAD001-All-RequireCompliantDeviceWindows-v1.0
policies.title: Require Compliant Windows Device
policies.description: Requires Intune-compliant Windows device for Office 365 access
policies.user_impact: high — non-compliant devices lose Office 365 access

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(e) — supply chain and endpoint security
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices; A.8.7 Protection Against Malware
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Establish and Maintain Secure Configuration Process; CIS 4.4 Implement and Manage Firewall Software
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: device compliance for data access
framework_controls.ncsc: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Secure configuration of all devices

policies.name: CAD002-All-RequireCompliantDeviceMacOS-v1.0
policies.title: Require Compliant macOS Device
policies.description: Requires Intune-compliant macOS device for Office 365 access
policies.user_impact: high

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(e) — endpoint security
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Secure Configuration

policies.name: CAD003-All-RequireCompliantOrAppProtectedMobile-v1.0
policies.title: Require Compliant or App-Protected Mobile Device
policies.description: Requires compliant device OR Intune app protection policy on iOS/Android
policies.user_impact: medium — personal devices need Intune Company Portal or MAM

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(e) — endpoint security including mobile
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Secure Configuration

policies.name: CAD004-All-BlockUnsupportedDevicePlatforms-v1.0
policies.title: Block Unsupported Device Platforms
policies.description: Blocks access from Linux, ChromeOS, and other unmanaged platforms
policies.user_impact: high for Linux/ChromeOS users

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(e) — endpoint security
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Secure Configuration

policies.name: CAD005-All-BlockDownloadsNonCompliantDevice-v1.0
policies.title: Block Downloads on Non-Compliant Devices
policies.description: Blocks file downloads via browser on non-compliant devices using MDCA session controls
policies.user_impact: medium — read-only browser access from unmanaged devices

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(h) — data protection
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.12 Data Leakage Prevention
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 3.6 Encrypt Data on End-User Devices

policies.name: CAD006-All-SignInFrequencyNonCompliantDevice-v1.0
policies.title: Session Timeout on Non-Compliant Devices
policies.description: Enforces 1-hour session timeout for access from non-compliant devices
policies.user_impact: medium — re-authentication required hourly from unmanaged devices

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — session management
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices; A.5.15 Access Control
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Secure Configuration

policies.name: CAD007-All-DisableBrowserPersistenceNonCompliant-v1.0
policies.title: Disable Browser Persistence on Non-Compliant Devices
policies.description: Prevents saving sessions on non-compliant devices
policies.user_impact: low

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — session controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.1 User Endpoint Devices
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 4.1 Secure Configuration

policies.name: CAL001-All-BlockHighRiskCountries-v1.0
policies.title: Block High Risk Countries
policies.description: Blocks sign-ins from countries the organisation does not operate in
policies.user_impact: medium — travellers to blocked countries affected
prerequisites: Named Location: blocked countries list configured

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(b) — threat intelligence and incident prevention
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.15 Access Control; A.8.16 Monitoring Activities
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 13.4 Perform Traffic Filtering
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: geo-blocking for unused regions

policies.name: CAL002-Admins-BlockNonTrustedLocations-v1.0
policies.title: Restrict Admin Access to Trusted Locations
policies.description: Blocks admin portal access from outside trusted office IP ranges
policies.user_impact: high — admins cannot use portals from home without VPN/trusted location
prerequisites: Named Location: trusted office IPs configured

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — privileged access controls and network segmentation
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.2 Privileged Access Rights; A.5.15 Access Control
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access; CIS 12.2 Establish and Maintain Secure Network Architecture
framework_controls.ncsc: NCSC admin guidance: restrict admin access to known-good locations

policies.name: CAL003-ServiceAccounts-RestrictToTrustedLocations-v1.0
policies.title: Restrict Service Accounts to Trusted Locations
policies.description: Locks service accounts to trusted IP ranges — service accounts should never sign in from unknown locations
policies.user_impact: none for human users

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — access control for automated accounts
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.16 Identity Management; A.5.18 Access Rights — service account controls
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 5.6 Centralise Account Management

policies.name: CAL004-All-RestrictMFARegistrationToTrustedLocations-v1.0
policies.title: Restrict MFA Registration to Trusted Locations
policies.description: Users can only register MFA methods from trusted office locations — prevents attackers registering their own MFA after initial compromise
policies.user_impact: medium — new employees must register MFA from office

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(i) — authentication registration controls
framework_controls.iso27001: A.5.17 Authentication Information
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 6.5 Require MFA for Administrative Access
framework_controls.microsoft_baseline: Microsoft recommended: restrict MFA registration to trusted locations

policies.name: CAU014-All-RouteThroughMDCANonCompliant-v1.0
policies.title: Route Non-Compliant Device Traffic Through MDCA
policies.description: Routes browser sessions from non-compliant devices through MDCA for DLP inspection and control
policies.user_impact: low — transparent to users, may affect performance slightly

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(h) — data protection; Article 21(2)(f) — network security
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.12 Data Leakage Prevention; A.8.16 Monitoring Activities
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 3.6 Encrypt Data; CIS 13.4 Perform Traffic Filtering

policies.name: CAU015-All-RouteThroughMDCACompliant-v1.0
policies.title: Route Compliant Device Traffic Through MDCA
policies.description: Routes all browser sessions (including compliant devices) through MDCA for full DLP coverage
policies.user_impact: low

framework_controls.nis2: Article 21(2)(h) — data protection
framework_controls.iso27001: A.8.12 Data Leakage Prevention
framework_controls.cis18: CIS 3.6 Encrypt Data; CIS 13.4 Traffic Filtering
